name: Tenacity
what_it_does: Python retry library with a simple decorator API. Tenacity provides @retry decorator with configurable retry conditions, stop strategies (max attempts, time limit), wait strategies (fixed, exponential backoff, random jitter), and callbacks (before, after, retry). Works with both sync and async functions. The standard retry library for Python — successor to retrying package.
use_cases: Add exponential backoff retry logic to API calls that may fail transiently (rate limits, network errors)
Retry database operations on connection failures with configurable max attempts and backoff
Use retry_if_exception_type to only retry on specific exception types (not programming errors)
Add before/after retry callbacks for logging and monitoring retry attempts
Use with async functions and asyncio with @retry decorator that handles coroutines
not_for: Distributed circuit breaking — use circuit breaker patterns (tenacity has some but not full circuit breaker)
Retry with complex state (retry different endpoints, fallback logic) — build custom retry logic for complex cases
Batch/queue-based retry — use Celery's retry mechanism for task-based retry patterns
best_when: You need simple, configurable retry logic for API calls or unreliable operations with exponential backoff in Python.
avoid_when: You need full circuit breaking, fallback URLs, or complex multi-step retry orchestration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Tenacity raises RetryError after max attempts, wrapping the last exception. before_sleep callbacks provide attempt number and exception details for logging. Statistics available via retry.statistics.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Retry assumes the wrapped function is idempotent — retrying non-idempotent operations (POST without idempotency key) may cause duplicate effects.
known_agent_gotchas: Tenacity retries on ANY exception by default — use retry=retry_if_exception_type(SomeError) to limit retries to expected transient errors; retrying ValueError or KeyError masks bugs
RetryError wraps the last exception — catch RetryError to handle exhausted retries, then access RetryError.__cause__ for the underlying exception
wait_exponential requires multiplier, min, and max parameters — without max, exponential backoff grows unboundedly; always set max wait time

Async support requires wrapping async functions — @retry decorator works on async def coroutines but the decorated function must be awaited; calling without await returns a coroutine object, not the result
The retry decorator is applied at import time — if retry configuration uses non-serializable objects (like lambda functions), it can cause issues with multiprocessing or celery
after=after_log(logger, logging.INFO) logs after every attempt including the first failure — use before_sleep for logging only retries, not the first attempt

security_score_components.security_notes: Retry library with no network access. Ensure retried functions are idempotent to prevent unintended side effects. Retry loops can amplify DDoS impact — include rate limit awareness in retry logic.
